body:
Bead: agent-9465287e
Outcome: no_changes — bug is on the ddx side of CONTRACT-003, not the agent side.

What was investigated
---------------------

The bead reports that a worker spawned with `ddx work --harness claude`
loses the `--harness claude` override on the post-REQUEST_CHANGES retry
iteration of execute-loop. The observable symptoms quoted in the bead:

    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=standard harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found
    kind:tier-attempt body=tier=smart    harness= model= probe=no viable provider, no viable harness found
    kind:escalation-summary winning_tier=exhausted

The bead description explicitly leaves the side-of-contract open and
asks for investigation to localize the fix.

Where the symptoms originate
----------------------------

I searched the entire agent repository for every distinguishing string
in the failure signature. None of them is produced by agent code:

    $ grep -rn "tier-attempt"                  --include="*.go" .   # 0 matches
    $ grep -rn "escalation-summary"            --include="*.go" .   # 0 matches
    $ grep -rn "no viable provider, no viable harness" --include="*.go" .  # 0 matches
    $ grep -rn "min-tier\|MinTier\|min_tier"   --include="*.go" .   # 0 matches

The only "no viable" string the agent emits is the routing engine's
internal "no viable routing candidate: %d candidates rejected"
(internal/routing/engine.go:206), which is wrapped by callers into
domain-specific errors elsewhere. The composite "no viable provider,
no viable harness found" message, the per-tier escalation loop, the
`tier-attempt` / `escalation-summary` event kinds, and the `min-tier`
adaptive-floor concept all live in ddx, not in this repo.

What the agent side does correctly
----------------------------------

`internal/routing/engine.go:292` (Resolve) implements the hard
harness-pin contract that the bead's first-attempt path relies on:

    // Hard harness override: skip non-matching harnesses.
    if canonicalHarness != "" && canonicalHarness != h.Name {
        continue
    }

When ddx hands the agent a request with `Harness: "claude"`, the
engine pins to that harness and ignores all others — exactly the
behavior observed on the bead's first attempt (which succeeds).
There is unit coverage for this in
`internal/routing/engine_test.go:1307` (TestHarnessOverrideRejectsOthers).

So the agent's routing engine is not rejecting the override; it is
never being asked. The retry path inside ddx's execute-loop is
constructing a service request with `Harness: ""` after the
REQUEST_CHANGES reopen, which then drops into ddx's adaptive
tier-escalation walk (with cheap floored out by min-tier) and exits
with the composite error.

Why no agent-side tuning is appropriate
---------------------------------------

The bead's third hypothesis ("agent's smart-routing rejects unpinned
candidates under min-tier=standard") does not apply: the agent has no
"tier" concept and no adaptive min-tier floor. Tier classification
and floor adaptation are ddx-side abstractions layered on top of the
agent's per-call routing. Loosening the agent's routing engine to
return candidates when the harness is empty would change behavior for
every other caller of CONTRACT-003 — it is not the right lever for a
per-bead retry-path bug.

Recommended fix location
------------------------

ddx-side, in the execute-loop reopen-retry path: when a bead reopens
after REQUEST_CHANGES (or any review-driven reopen), the loop must
copy the worker-level harness override (and any other explicit pins
like model/profile) into the next iteration's ExecuteBeadOptions /
service.Execute request, the same way it does on the initial claim.
The agent's CONTRACT-003 surface is already correct.

What a follow-up attempt would need
-----------------------------------

Re-file this bead against the ddx repository (where cli/main.go and
the execute-loop live, per the bead description's reference to
"ddx v0.6.2-alpha1 cli/main.go"), with reproduction steps targeting
the reopen-retry path. The fix is a few lines of option-propagation
in execute-loop and a regression test that asserts the second
service.Execute call carries the same Harness value as the first
when the bead was reopened by a reviewer.

No code changes were made in this worktree.
